RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed by Intervenor Continental Insurance Company with Joinder by Intervenors Harford Accident and Indemnity Company and Hartford Insurance Company of the Midwest. Plaintiff has demonstrated that his health is such that a preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation.
The trial date is November 1, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is November 10, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 28, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Darrel Grace.
Lori A. Cataldo of FOLEY & MANSFIELD LLP (510-590-9500) appearing for defendant C.C.M. Corporation.

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted:

DEFENDANT C.C.M CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing the Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Plaintiff testified that Meeks Lumber trucks delivered materials including joint compound to his jobsites in the 1970s, including a tavern/motel in Grenada, California, a Yreka High School project, a tract home project off Turre Street and the Camel tract project off Oberlin Street. (Darrell Grace Deposition [Heidarzadeh Decl. Ex. D and Rose Decl. Ex. B at 572-575, 578, 580, 583, 585, 587-88].) The declaration of William C. Meek is insufficient to establish that Meeks Lumber did not made any deliveries to Plaintiff's jobsites in Yreka in the 1970s as it does not address other store locations in the surrounding area. (Heidarzadeh Decl. Ex. F.)
